text.title: Google Performance Max Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding the google-pmax methodology. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: PMax MUST NOT be enabled below 30 conversions/month at the account level.
rule.rationale: PMax requires conversion signal to optimize; below 30/mo it wastes spend in unobservable buckets.

rule.statement: Each PMax campaign MUST have ≥3 asset groups partitioned by theme (audience or product line).
rule.rationale: Single asset group makes Google's blender opaque + un-diagnosable.

rule.statement: Each asset group MUST attach audience signals (custom segments + customer match + remarketing) for cold-start acceleration.
rule.rationale: Without signals PMax explores broadly; with signals it converges in 1-2 weeks instead of 4-6.

rule.statement: Brand keywords MUST be added as account-level negatives on PMax to prevent cannibalization of brand Search campaigns.
rule.rationale: Otherwise PMax shows up on brand queries at higher CPA than dedicated brand Search.

rule.statement: Conversion events used by PMax MUST be assigned value priority (Purchase > Lead > SignUp) or all-or-nothing primary event marking.
rule.rationale: Equal-weight events skew toward low-value conversions.
